dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to South [CITY] for breathing problems at an assisted living home.
Hello, I'm Jordan, a paramedic with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I've had trouble breathing since I smoked a cigarette about an hour ago.
Can you tell me your name, the date and time, and the location we're at now?
My name is Matthew, and I'm in an assisted living home in [CITY].
What is today's date and day of the week?
It's November 3rd, and today is a Wednesday.
Do you understand what's happening and why we're here?
Yes, I'm aware - I'm just struggling to breathe well. I have been coughing up green mucus too.
Are you able to speak and cough freely, or is your airway blocked?
Respirations are at 16 and labored with wheezing. There is use of accessory muscles.
You mentioned trouble breathing. Are you experiencing hypoxia, like your lips or fingers turning blue?
Pulse is 112 and strong. Blood pressure is 193/118. Skin is warm, dry, and intact.
Skin is warm and dry with intact condition. No bite marks or rashes reported.
Eyes are reactive. No signs of trauma or bleeding. No known JVD.
I need to check for trauma. Can you move your head and neck without pain?
Yes, I think I'm fine. No pain in my head or neck.
You're using accessory muscles to breathe - that shows increased respiratory effort.
Can you tell me what medications you take?
I use Advair for COPD and Albuterol for asthma on a regular basis.
Any other medications or allergies?
I take aspirin for heart issues, Omeprazole for GERD, and Haloperidol for my mental health. I also have Cardizem, Tylenol, Guaifenesin, and Divalproex Sodium.
Any other medical conditions you'd like to tell me about?
I have COPD, asthma, primary hypertension, GERD, and Schizoaffective Disorder. I also take Benztropine and Motrin.
What has your breathing been like in the days before this?
I've been coughing up green mucus for a couple days now - it wasn't clear.
On a scale of 0 to 10, how would you rate your difficulty breathing today?
Maybe a 6 or 7. Not severe pain, but still hard to breathe.
Your pulse is 112 and consistent, but trending upward.
Breathing is at 16, shallow with some fatigue showing.
Blood pressure is 193/118. Heart rate continues to rise.
Oxygen saturation is 42 - not improving with initial oxygen.
EKG shows sinus tachycardia. Your history includes bradycardia.
You've declined a glucose check at this time.
We are giving you 15LPM oxygen to support your breathing.
Setting up a nebulizer with albuterol and ipratropium now.
CPAP is available and will be placed if needed for increased support.
First dose of Albuterol and Atrovent has been administered.
Dexamethasone 10mg IM given to help reduce airway effects.
We're following the respiratory distress protocol for COPD and asthma exacerbation.
Let's check your breathing after the first dose of medication.
Pulse now is 116, respirations 22, and BP 179/100 - breathing sounds are improving.
Oxygen saturation has improved to 86, showing a positive initial response.
CPAP is being activated - let's begin using it now.
We've delivered a second dose of Albuterol, it's now in the nebulizer.
Pulse is now 140, respirations 20, and BP 177/115. Heart rate is continuing to elevate.
4-lead ECG and capnography are in place for continuous monitoring.
Where would you like to go for further breathing support?
I would like to get to [COMPANY_NAME].
You're secured in the stretcher, in a semi Fowler's position.
Hospital has been called - we're heading there urgently.
CPAP is active - you're breathing a little easier now.
Second nebulizer is ready and now in progress.
Oxygen improved to 86, and breathing appears more stable now.
All secured. Let's make sure we have a smooth ride.
You've declined glucose testing, and you also opted not to get an IV.
You're awake and breathing better. We are monitoring closely.
No reported pain overall and vital signs are steady for now.
All procedures completed and in line with the COPD protocol.
Bed is ready at [COMPANY_NAME] - CPAP remains in place.
Patient delivered to medical bed 7 using a draw sheet.
Nurse has signed off - we've transitioned care to their team.
You were great - receiving more attention now at the hospital.
Thanks for the help - will call if I don't improve.
Nurse received report - all equipment is being used with transport vitals ongoing.
Your GCS is 15 - alert and oriented with no pain reported.
